name: action
description: portitor's GitHub-action mediation: the actions portitor performs on the upstream with its own credential (PR open/comment/review/merge/close + read-side fetch), and the SSH forced-command that dispatches a connection to either the git pack commands or the role-gated action API. The agent never holds a GitHub credential and never runs gh; portitor re-derives every action's authority from verified git state or the caller's role. NOTE: hand-authored SEED in spexmachina's format; identity hashes are generated when run through spex.

requirements.title: No gh passthrough — constrained mediation only
requirements.description: Every GitHub action is performed by portitor as a consequence of verified git state or as a narrow, role-validated operation whose target portitor can tie back to managed work. gh arguments are constructed by portitor (internal/action), never forwarded from the agent, so the agent cannot run arbitrary gh.

requirements.title: Auto-open a PR when a feature branch is forwarded
requirements.description: On post-receive, after an accepted non-default branch is forwarded upstream, portitor opens a PR itself: head = the gated branch, base = the default branch, title from the branch's tip commit subject, body from the branch's commit messages (git log default..branch). It is not an agent command. Idempotent: if an open PR already exists for the branch (e.g. a self-correction re-push), the existing number is returned instead of opening a duplicate.

requirements.title: Role-gated pr action API (config-driven, default-deny)
requirements.description: `portitor pr <comment|review|merge|close|fetch> --pr N` performs one action with the upstream credential after checking the caller's role against the per-repo config: action_roles maps each verb of the closed mechanism set (fetch|comment|review|merge|close) to the roles allowed to invoke it, and an action not listed for a role is refused — default-deny, since every action is privileged. Role names are free-form opaque strings (no built-in role set); validate-config rejects unknown verbs in action_roles. Bodies (comment/review text) are read from stdin so multi-line markdown survives transport. fetch returns PR review state JSON (the read-side that feeds review/fix bundles).

requirements.title: Merge preconditions are re-derived from authoritative state
requirements.description: Before merging, portitor re-derives every precondition in one gh query plus the local repo, refusing with the full unmet list: reviewDecision == APPROVED; mergeStateStatus == CLEAN (mandatory — covers behind-base, conflicts, and blocked); every configured required_check present and successful in statusCheckRollup (an empty required_checks list is advisory); and separation of duties — the requesting key must not have signed any commit the PR introduces (verified against the local gated repo via rev-list default..head + %GF, fail-closed if the head ref is absent locally; the same check guards review --event approve). Enforcement is hybrid: the final gh pr merge is the atomic gate (GitHub re-checks, closing the TOCTOU window). The landing credential is optional — with no role granted merge in action_roles, merge is unavailable via portitor.

requirements.title: Durable audit trail of gate and action decisions
requirements.description: Every L1 gate decision (accept/reject/error, malformed hook stdin included), every forward outcome, every L2 action decision (allow/deny/error with the reason), and every auto-open outcome appends one fsync'd JSON line to the config's audit_log path (file 0600, missing parents 0700). Kinds: gate, forward, action, auto-pr. Fields: time, kind, repo, caller fingerprint + role where known (the shell dispatcher exports the fingerprint to the hook environment for push attribution), action/PR/refs, verdict, reason. A config that cannot be loaded is the one inherently unauditable failure. Unset audit_log disables the trail; a write failure never changes a verdict — it is loudly reported to stderr (deliberate, visible trade-off so an audit-disk problem cannot block landing work).

requirements.title: SSH forced-command dispatch (portitor shell)
requirements.description: `portitor shell <fingerprint>` is the forced command on an agent key's authorized_keys entry. It classifies SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND against a closed table: git-receive-pack/upload-pack '<path>' (confined to the repo root, *.git, no traversal) execs the real git command so the pre/post-receive gate runs, with the caller fingerprint exported to the subprocess environment for audit attribution; `portitor pr ...` runs the role-gated action API with the role mapped from the key fingerprint; everything else — including git-upload-archive — is rejected. One key thus grants gated git + the narrow action API and nothing more.

requirements.title: Routing is a pure, testable function
requirements.description: The security-critical dispatch (classify), role policy (action.RoleCan over the config's action_roles, default-deny), merge-precondition evaluation (a pure function over the fetched state), repo-path confinement (allowedRepoPath), command tokenizing (shellSplit), and slug derivation (deriveSlug) are pure functions, unit-tested without a network. The gh layer (internal/action) is tested with a stubbed runner.

components.name: ActionClient
components.description: internal/action.GH: typed wrappers over gh (OpenPR, OpenPRNumber, Comment, Review, Merge, ClosePR, Fetch, FetchMergeState) pinned to one repo slug via -R, plus the pure precondition evaluator UnmetMergePreconditions; a swappable Runner makes it testable with stubbed gh.

components.name: AutoOpenPR
components.description: post-receive opens a PR per forwarded feature branch (title from tip commit, body from commit messages), idempotently, and prints the receipt.

components.name: PrCommand
components.description: `portitor pr` handler: parses the action + --pr/--event, checks action.RoleCan against the config's action_roles (default-deny), reads the body from stdin, and dispatches to ActionClient; merge re-derives the full precondition set (approval, CLEAN merge state, required checks, separation of duties) and review-approve re-derives separation of duties; every decision is appended to the audit trail.

components.name: ShellDispatcher
components.description: `portitor shell <fingerprint>`: classify() routes SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND to git pack (exec, gate runs) or the role-gated pr API (role from fingerprint), rejecting anything else; allowedRepoPath confines pack commands.
